statement.en: HUMAN CAPITAL VALUATION AS A STRATEGIC FINANCIAL ASSET: INTEGRATING INTANGIBLE WORKFORCE METRICS INTO FINANCIAL REPORTING IN THE IT SECTOR
statement.summary: ABSTRACT Human capital has emerged as a decisive driver of competitive advantage in the information technology (IT) sector, yet its strategic value remains largely underrepresented within conventional financial reporting frameworks. This paper explores the integration of intangible workforce metrics into financial valuation models, proposing a comprehensive approach for recognizing human capital as a measurable and strategic financial asset. Drawing on contemporary organizational theory, intellectual capital frameworks, and sectorspecific dynamics, the study evaluates how workforce capabilities—such as technical proficiency, innovation capacity, and organizational adaptability—contribute to long-term corporate performance. It further examines the gaps within existing accounting standards t

proposer: G. Vandana, D. Lekita, S. Pavani

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom proposes a novel integration framework (human capital as financial asset) that is theoretically motivated but lacks empirical validation across heterogeneous IT contexts. The paper appears to be a preprint (2026-04-25) presenting a provisional model under development. Human capital valuation remains contested in accounting theory—some frameworks support it (e.g., Lev's IC framework), others resist it on measurement grounds. The proposition is not yet refuted but contingent on standardization adoption and empirical testing.
